clinical_criteria: Cases: Any person with a cough noted as an allergy or adverse event associated with ACEi use
Controls: Patients on an ACEi for at least 6 months without a documented allergy/adverse reaction of cough
Case exclusions: 1) No history of ACEi use, 2) No history of cough associated with ACEi

Control exclusions: 1) Ever switched from ACEi to ARB, 2) Any report of adverse effects including cough, angioedema, shortness of breath, dyspnea associated with ACEi, 3) On ACEi for less than 6 months
algorithm_summary: This phenotype identifies ACE inhibitor-induced cough cases and controls. Cases are defined as patients with documented cough as an allergy or adverse event associated with ACE inhibitor use. Controls are patients on ACE inhibitors for at least 6 months without documented cough adverse reactions and who have not been switched to ARBs. The algorithm excludes cases without ACEi history or cough association, and excludes controls who were switched to ARBs, had any ACEi adverse effects, or used ACEi for less than 6 months. The phenotype was validated through chart review of 50 cases and 50 controls and used in GWAS studies that identified KCNIP4 as associated with this phenotype.
